name: Weapon +1
description: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

name: Weapon +2
description: You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

name: Weapon +3
description: You have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

name: Weapon of Warning
description: This magic weapon warns you of danger. While the weapon is on your person, you have advantage on initiative rolls. In addition, you and any of your companions within 30 feet of you can’t be surprised, except when incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within range if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.

name: Armor of Gleaming
description: This armor never gets dirty.

name: Cast-Off Armor
description:
Cast-Off Armor - Padded
You can doff this armor as an action.
Xanathar's Guide to Everything p136

name: Moon-Touched Sword
description: In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.

name: Shield of Expressions
description:
The front of this shield is shaped in the likeness of a face. While bearing the shield, you can use a bonus action to alter the faces expression.
Xanathar's Guide to Everything p139

name: Smoldering Armor
description:
Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.
Xanathar's Guide to Everything p139

name: Walloping Ammunition
description:
This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.
Xanathar's Guide to Everything p139

name: All or Nothing Coin
description: This appears to be a common gold coin. While you are able to flip the coin (you have a hand free, and the coin is accessible), you can choose to flip the coin instead of rolling a d20. If you land on Heads, it is a critical success (Natural 20). If you hand on tails, it is a critical fail (Natural 1). The coin can be used in this way once per short rest.

name: Dust of De-Appearance
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
Can make things invisible, but wears off if the object moves, is moved, or even wiggles a bit. Best used on inanimate objects or people who can hold really, really, still.

name: Paired Scrolls
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
As long as the scrolls are on the same plane, whatever is written on one scroll, appears on the other. Each scrolls can "transmit" up to two sentences before becoming inactive for 24 hours. After 24 hours, anything written on the scrolls fade.

name: Mighty Acorn
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
When thrown on a patch of earth, at least 5 feet wide, it immediately grows into a 20' oak.

name: Silver Handkerchief
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
A 12 inch piece of silver fabric. When a command word is spoken, the fabric immediately flattens and becomes as hard as steel. A second command word reverts the handkerchief back to the fabric form.

name: Ring of Tracking
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
A simple ring, when attuned to a user, the user knows the location of the ring at all times. Often used by merchants and collectors to keep track of their goods.

name: Shepherd's Staff
description: While wielding the staff, the holder can dominate beast once per day with a DC 13 wisdom save. Only works on farm animals.

name: Dagger of Whittling
description: A finely made halfling dagger. This dagger can be used to whittle a 6" piece of wood into a small object once per day, without any DC check. The dagger can carve small toys, figures and other items made of wood (worth 1d10 gp).

name: Bottomless Mug
description: A masterwork stone mug, of the highest quality. Despite being normal sized, the mug can hold 10 gallons or 40 liters of liquid.

name: Everburning Torch
description:
This item appears to be identical to a regular torch. However, when you speak a command word it lights up with a green flame and continues burning until you speak another command word. The torch provides bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. If you make a melee attack with a burning torch and hit, it deals 1 fire damage. Note: This is basically a torch with a modified
continual flame
(level 2) spell cast upon it.

name: Wand of Illumination
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you cause the wand to emit light as a light spell centered on the wand. As a bonus action, you can cause the wand’s light to leap from the wand and move about or even split into multiple light sources, as dancing lights. These functions of the wand do not require charges.
As an action, you can expend 1 of its charges to create daylight, which is centered on the wand and moves with it. If you expend 2 of its charges, you can cause the daylight to appear anywhere within 60 feet.
As an action, you can expend 4 of the wand’s charges to create a sunbeam (save DC 15). This functions like the spell but does not require concentration and creates only a single beam of radiance.
The wand regains 1d6+1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand’s last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand disintegrates into tiny glowing golden motes and is destroyed.

name: Mask of Fraz-Urb'luu
description:
This mask is crafted from the head of the Demon Lord of Illusions, Fraz-Urb'luu. The head looks like that of a terrifying gargoyle, with horrifying and hypnotic gaze.
While wearing this mask, you gain the following benefits:
You get a +2 to Strength (Intimidation) checks, and a -2 to Charisma (Persuasion) checks.
You have advantage on any Intelligence (Investigation) to see through an illusion.
Once per day, you can create a terrifying illusion. Any creature within a 15' cone, that you can see, must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a fail, the creature takes 3d10 psychic damage, and is frightened of you until the end of its next turn.
Note:
The Mask of Fraz-Urb'luu is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated Fraz-Urb'luu (a high level demon lord).

name: Demogorgon Helm
description:
The Demogorgon Helm is a metallic helm with blue and red accents, that is shaped like the head of a mandrill. The helm is magically crafted using the eyes of Demogorgon, the Prince of Demons (which can only be harvested once he is killed). The pupils of the eyeballs are surgically removed, magically treated, and then crafted into smaller lenses, which are then worked into a magically crafted helm. Once complete, the helm retains some of the power behind Demogorgon's beguiling gaze.
While wearing the helm, you get the following powers:
You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.
Once per day, you can use an action to activate the helm's gaze effect. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a fail, the target suffers one of the following effects (roll a d6).
1-2 : Beguiling Gaze.
The target is stunned until the end of its next turn.
3-4 : Confusing Gaze.
The target suffers the effect of the
confusion
spell until the end of its next turn.
5-6 : Hypnotic Gaze.
The target suffers the effect of the charmed condition until the end of its next turn.
Note:
Demogorgon Helm is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this item should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated Demogorgon (a high level demon lord).

name: Graz'zt's Charm
description:
While wearing this pendant, you get advantage on any roles that may cause you to become charmed.
While wearing this pendant, you get a +2 to persuasion checks.
This pendant starts with 1d3+1 charges. While wearing the pendant, you can use a charge to cast one of the following spells: Friends, Charm Person, Tasha's Hideous Laughter. The pendant regains 1 charge per day, and can never exceed 4 charges.
Note:
Graz'zt's charm is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated Graz'zt (a high level demon lord).

name: Jubilex Gem
description:
The Jubilex Gem is a powerful magic item that can only be obtained by killing and harvesting ooze from the powerful demon Jubilex. Once this ooze is collected from Jubilex, a powerful spellcaster must spend a week performing rituals to harden and shape the ooze into a dark green gem-like stone. Even though this gem is solid, it looks like it is filled with a gelatinous substance that is constantly swirling around. The gem can be set into a pendant, or attached to a necklace.
While holding or wearing this gem, you gain the following benefits:
You gain advantage on saving throws against being poisoned
You can move through spaces meant for creatures two sizes smaller (Example: Medium-sized creatures can normally squeeze through Small spaces. This gems would allow a Medium creature to squeeze through a Tiny space).
Once per day, using an action, you can make your body amorphous like an ooze. For 10 minutes, you can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch wide without squeezing.
Note:
The Jubilex Gem is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful demon Jubilex.

name: Mace of the Fallen Angel
description:
The mace has the following abilites:
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this mace.
3 times a day, you can add an additional 2d10 fire damage to an attack with this weapon.
While wielding the mace, you gain resistance to fire damage.
Note:
The Mace of the Fallen Angel is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this item should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated Zariel (a high level archdevil).

name: Fang of Yeenoghu
description:
The Fang of Yeenoghu is a powerful magical item that can only be obtained by killing and harvesting two items from the demon lord Yeenoghu — a canine tooth and the creature's amygdala. The tooth needs to be polish, cleaned, and magically prepared. The amygdala must be soaked in an alchemical mixture for a week where it hardens into a reddish stone. The stone is then polished with a magical residue, resulting in a dark red pearl. The tooth and the pearl are then combined during a ritual under a full moon. The final item is added to a chain or string, and worn as a necklace.
While wearing this necklace, you gain the following benefits:
You have advantage to Wisdom (Survival) checks when tracking creatures.
You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.
On the first turn of combat, you can move double your base movement speed.
When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack, you can use a bonus action to move up to half your speed and make another melee attack.
Note:
The Fang of Yeenoghu is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful demon Yeenoghu.

name: Horn of Orcus
description:
The Horn of Orcus is a powerful magical item that can only be obtained by killing Orcus and harvesting his horns. Once harvested, only one of his horns retains enough power to create this magic item. The horn needs to be hollowed out and magically treated, before a week long ritual can be performed to tap into the necrotic magic within. Note: No
good
character would willing perform this ritual.
While holding the horn, you have the following abilities:
You are immune to contracting any disease
You have resistance to necrotic damage
You can cast
chill touch
at will
3 times per day, you can cast one of the following spells:
False Life (cast as a level 1 spell)
Animate Dead (cast as a level 3 spell)
Speak with Dead
Note:
The Horn of Orcus is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful demon lord Orcus.

name: Helm of Baphomet
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
The Helm of Baphomet is a powerful magical item that can only be obtained by killing Baphomet (in the Abyss) and harvesting his horns. The process to create the helm involves the forging of a magical helm to hold and harness the horn's power. The helm looks like a goats head made of steel, with two large horns protruding from the top of the head.
While wearing the helm, you have the following abilities:
You have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision. wearing the goggles increases its range by 60 feet.
You have advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks while attempting to navigation through a maze or complex dungeon (DM discretion).
Gore.
You can use your horned helm to make an unarmed strike at a target within 5 feet. If you hit with them, you deal pericing damage equal to 1d8 + your Strength modifier.
Charge.
If you move at least 10 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra 9 (2d8) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 10 feet away and knocked prone.
Note:
The Helm of Baphomet is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this helm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful demon lord Baphomet.

name: Geryon's Sting
description:
While wielding this dagger, you have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.
In addition, you gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. You can use a bonus to cause thick, black poison to coat the blade. The poison remains for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or take 2d10 poison damage and become poisoned for 1 minute. The dagger has 3 charges, all of which are recharged at dawn.
Note:
Geryon's Sting is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful devil Geryon.

name: Hutijin's Harp
description:
A creature that attempts to play the instrument without being attuned to it must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take 2d4 psychic damage.
While holding the instrument, you have resistance to fire damage. In addition, you can use an action to play the instrument and cast one of the following spells:
Darkness
Fear
Flame Arrows
Wall of Fire
Once the instrument has been used to cast a spell, it can't be used to cast that spell again until the next dawn. The spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.
Note:
Hutijin's Harp is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this charm should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful devil Hutijin.

name: Moloch's Hell Hammer
description:
While wielding the warhammer, you get the following powers:
You get a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls using this warhammer.
The warhammer has 9 charges. When you hit with a melee attack using it, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, the target takes an extra 1d6 fire damage. The warhammer regains 1d6 + 3 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the warhammer loses this one ability. The warhammer can never exceed more than 9 charges.
You have resistance to fire damage.
You can speak, read, and write Infernal.
Note:
Moloch's Hell Hammer is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this item should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated Moloch (a high level archdevil).

name: Titivilus' Collar
description:
While wearing this collar, you benefit from the following effects:
You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.
You get a +2 to any persuasion check.
Twisting Words:
Once per day, you can use an action to target one creature that you can see within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or become
charmed
for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Any attack on the target also ends this effect.
Frightful Words:
Once per day, you can use an action to target one creature that you can see within 30 feet. The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or become
frightened
for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
Note:
Titivilus' Collar is a powerful magic item, that should only be given to powerful characters. As such, this item should only be awarded to a high level party that has defeated the powerful devil Titivilus.

name: Bookmark (Dagger)
description:
This +3 dagger belongs to Artus Cimber (see Tome of Annilihation, Appendix D). While you have the dagger drawn, you can use a bonus action to activate one of the following properties:
Cause a blue gem set into the dagger's pommel to shed bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet, or make the gem go dark.
Turn the dagger into a compass that, while resting on your palm, points north.
Cast
dimension door
from the dagger. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Cast
compulsion
(save DC 15) from the dagger. The range of the spell increases to 90 feet, but it targets only spiders that are beasts. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
Tome of Annihilation p195

name: Spelljammer Helm
description:
The function of this ornate chair is to propel and maneuver a ship on which it has been installed through space and air. It can also propel and maneuver a ship on water or underwater, provided the ship is built for such travel. The ship in question must weigh 1 ton or more.
The sensation of being attuned to a
spelljamming helm
is akin to the pins-and-needles effect one experiences after one's arm or leg falls asleep, but not painful.
While attuned to a
spelljamming helm
and sitting in it, you gain the following abilities for as long as you maintain concentration (as if concentrating on a spell):
You can use the
spelljamming helm
to move the ship through space, air, or water up to the ship's speed. If the ship is in space and no other objects weighing 1 ton or more are within 1 mile of it, you can use the spelljamming helm to move the vessel fast enough to travel 100 million miles in 24 hours.
You can steer the vessel, albeit in a somewhat clumsy fashion, in much the way a rudder or oars can be used to maneuver a seafaring ship.
At any time, you can see and hear what's happening on and around the vessel as though you were standing in a location of your choice aboard it.
Transfer Attunement.
You can use an action to touch a willing spellcaster. That creature attunes to the
spelljamming helm
immediately, and your attunement to it ends.
Spelljammer Adventures in Space

name: Crystal Blade
description:
This magic sword's blade is fashioned from a horn or spine from a crystal dragon. When you hit with an attack roll using this sword, the target takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.
The sword has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you hit a creature with an attack roll using the sword, you can expend 1 charge to regain a number of hit points equal to the extra radiant damage the sword dealt.
While you're holding the sword, you can use a bonus action to cause it to shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet, to cause it to shed dim light in a 10-foot radius, or to douse the light.
Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p22

name: Dragonlance
description:
A dragonlance is a renowned weapon forged from rare metal with the aid of powerful artifacts. On Krynn, its creation is associated with the god Paladine and legendary heroes who fought against the evil of the Dragon Queen. Different lances are forged for use by foot soldiers (as pikes) and by riders (as lances), but the magical properties of the weapons are the same.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
When you hit a Dragon with this weapon, the Dragon takes an extra 3d6 force damage, and any Dragon of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you can immediately use its reaction to make a melee attack.
Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p23

name: Dragon Wing Bow
description:
The limb tips of this magic bow are shaped like a dragon's wings, and the weapon is infused with the essence of a chromatic, gem, or metallic dragon's breath. When you hit with an attack roll using this magic bow, the target takes an extra 1d6 damage of the same type as the breath infused in the bow—acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant, or thunder.
If you load no ammunition in the weapon, it produces its own, automatically creating one piece of magic ammunition when you pull back the string. The ammunition created by the bow vanishes the instant after it hits or misses a target.
Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p23

name: Figurine of Wondrous Power, Gold Canary
description:
This figurine of wondrous power is a gold statuette of a canary and is small enough to fit in a pocket. If you use an action to speak the command word and throw the figurine to a point on the ground within 60 feet of you, the figurine becomes a living creature in one of two forms (you choose). If there isn't enough space for the creature where it would appear, the figurine doesn't become a creature. The two forms are as follows:
Giant Canary Form.
The figurine becomes a
giant canary
for up to 8 hours and can be ridden as a mount. Once the figurine has become a giant canary, it can't be used this way again until the next dawn.
Gold Dragon Form.
While you are missing half or more of your hit points, you can speak a different command word and the figurine becomes an
adult gold dragon
for up to 1 hour. The dragon can't use any legendary actions or lair actions. Once the figurine has become an adult gold dragon, it can't be used this way again until 1 year has passed.
In either form, the creature is friendly to you and your companions. It understands your languages and obeys your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, the creature defends itself but takes no other actions.
The creature exists for a duration specific to each figurine. At the end of the duration, the creature reverts to its figurine form. It reverts to a figurine early if it drops to 0 hit points or if you use an action to speak the command word again while touching it. When the creature becomes a figurine again, its property can't be used again until a certain amount of time has passed, as specified in the figurine's description.
Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p23

name: Potion of Dragon Majesty
description:
This potion looks like liquid gold, with a single scale from a chromatic, gem, or metallic dragon suspended in it. When you drink this potion, you transform into an adult dragon of the same kind as the dragon the scale came from. The transformation lasts for 1 hour. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying melds into your new form or falls to the ground (your choice). For the duration, you use the game statistics of the adult dragon instead of your own, but you retain your languages, personality, and memories. You can’t use a dragon’s Change Shape or its legendary or lair actions.
Fizban's Treasury of Dragons p24

name: Chromatic Rose
description:
This magic rose comes in one of five colors, as noted in the Chromatic Roses table. While a rose is held, it gains a harmless visual effect as indicated on the table.
While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to damage of the type associated with the rose's color. If you would take more than 10 damage of this type from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no damage instead.
As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of acid, lightning, poisonous gas, fire, or cold, as dictated by the rose's damage type. Each creature in the cone must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw, taking 3d10 damage of the appropriate type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.
Chromatic Roses
Color
Visual Effect
Damage Type
Black
Drips acid
Acid
Blue
Crackles with Lightning
Lightning
Green
Issues green gas
Poison
Red
Wreathed in fire
Fire
White
Covered with Frost
Cold
The Wild Beyond the Witchlight p208

name: Armor of Safeguarding
description:
Set in the center of this armor's chest is a citrine engraved with the shield rune.
While wearing this armor, your hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to 10 + your level.
Invoking the Rune.
As an action, you can invoke the armor's rune to cast the
beacon of hope
spell with it; the spell has a duration of 1 minute and doesn't require concentration. Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Base items.
This item variant can be applied to the following base items:
Chain Mail (Chain Mail of Safeguarding)
Plate Armor (Plate Armor of Safeguarding)
Ring Mail (Ring Mail of Safeguarding)
Splint Armor (Splint Armor of Safeguarding)
Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants p111

name: Bloodshed Blade
description:
The hilt of this sword bears a carnelian engraved with the blood rune.
You can add your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to the damage rolls of attacks made with this weapon.
Invoking the Rune.
When you target a creature with an attack using this weapon, you can invoke the sword's rune, causing it to flare with crimson light and infusing your attack with bloodthirsty precision. You then spend and roll one of your unspent Hit Dice and add the number rolled to the attack roll. You can choose to invoke the rune after rolling the d20.
If this attack hits, you can also spend and roll any number of your unspent Hit Dice and add the total rolled to the weapon's damage.
Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Base items.
This item variant can be applied to the following base items:
Double-Bladed Scimitar (Bloodshed Double-Bladed Scimitar)
Greatsword (Bloodshed Greatsword)
Longsword (Bloodshed Longsword)
Rapier (Bloodshed Rapier)
Scimitar (Bloodshed Scimitar)
Shortsword (Bloodshed Shortsword)
Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants p111

name: Zephyr Armor
description:
This fine set of white-and-silver armor bears the wind rune upon its chest.
While wearing this armor, you have advantage on Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks and Dexterity saving throws as your movements are bolstered by gentle currents of wind.
Invoking the Rune.
As an action, you can invoke the armor's rune to cast the
wind wall
spell (save DC 15) with it. Once the rune has been invoked, it can't be invoked again until the next dawn.
Base items.
This item variant can be applied to the following base items:
Leather Armor (Zephyr Leather Armor)
Padded Armor (Zephyr Padded Armor)
Studded Leather Armor (Zephyr Studded Leather Armor)
Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants p117

name: Spoon of Stirring
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a simple, wooden spoon, about 18 inches long with the handle engraved with circular swirling patterns. When someone starts to stir a bowl, pot or cauldron, the spoon magically begins to stir by itself. The mouth of the bowl, pot or cauldron must be less than 2 feet wide, and the spoon must be dipped in at least 1 inch of liquid for it to work. The spoon stirs in a slow, circular pattern. It stops stirring when someone pulls the spoon out of the liquid, or when there is less than an inch of liquid left in the bowl. The spoon is destroyed if dipped in anything over 500 degrees Fahrenheit (276 degrees Celsius) [such as molten iron, or lava].

name: Broom of Sweeping
description:
This object resembles a common broom, made from a small bundle of straw tied to a slightly crooked wooden handle. The broom is often mistaken for a non-magical broom, but has a small infinity symbol engraved at the top of it's handle. 
When a command word is spoken, the broom will "stand up" (if it's not already) and begin to sweep an area no larger than 90 square feet. The broom will move gently, as if someone was casually sweeping the area. It will move around corners and up slight inclines, but will not go up stairs, nor push objects over 5 lbs. The broom will also avoid liquids deeper than 1 inch (and will not work if placed in deeper bodies of water). The broom is deactivated when the command word is spoken again, or someone grabs the handle. 
The broom is quite fragile, and can be snapped like a sturdy stick (destroying it's magic) or a tree branch. Temperature of 600°F destroyed the broom (it will not sweep lava or molten iron).

name: Rag of Cleaning
description:
The rag can be used to wipe down (dm discretion):
-- semi-clean 30'x30' area
-- a moderately dirty 5'x5' area
-- 1'x1' extremely dirty area
After such use, it becomes covered in filth (and is unusable until it's "cleaned").

name: Bowl of Warming
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is an iron bowl, about a foot in diameter, engraved with decorative swooshes of both fire and water. The bowl can hold about 2 cups (24 ounces of liquid). When liquid is placed into the bowl, and it is held firmly by two hands, it takes 2 rounds to heat the liquid. The bowl itself remains cool to the touch, but the liquid can reach 212 °F (100 °C) - the boiling point of water.

name: Stones of Cooling
description:
These usually come in pairs. They are two smooth, gray stone rocks, carved into perfect 1-inch cubes, with slightly rounded edges. When these rocks are placed into a liquid container, no larger than 32 oz, the liquid cools down to 40°F and 42°F (4-6°C) [the temperature of a cool drink with ice]. If placed in larger amounts of water, the stones do not work at all.
Note: A waterskin is 4 pints (64 ounces), so it would need to be half full for the cubes to work.

name: Mug of Second Servings
description: This is a brass mug with a handle, that can hold 14 ounces of fluid. When the mug is filled with at least 12 ounces of a non-magical, consumable a liquid, and the someone drinks all of the contents (it can not be poured out), then the mug magically refills with the same liquid within 6 seconds. Once the mug is magically refilled, it will not refill until another 12 ounces of liquid are poured in. The mug can function in this way 10 times a day.

name: Neverdull Blade
description: This is a simple blade, that always has a polished shine and never gets dull. The blade has no combat benefits, but never needs to be polished or sharpened with a whetstone. The type of blade is determined by the DM.

name: Everdry Book Cover
description: This is a leather sheath that slides over the covers of a book. This item protects a book from water and acid damage, even when the book is completely submerged. The book can even be opened while submerged, and will be protected from water and acid as long as the book cover remains in place. The book cover stretches/shrinks as needed. However, it will not fit on any book with a cover larger than 2 feet tall or wide, or any book with a cover smaller than 6 inches tall or wide. The book cover does not protect from fire or lava. This item was created by sea elves to transfer knowledge from the surface to underwater realms.

name: Fireproof Book Cover
description: This is a leather sheath that slides over the covers of a book. This item protects a book from heat and fire damage. Even when open, the book be protected from fire (and even lava) as long as the book cover remains in place. The bookcover stretches/shrinks as needed. However, it will not fit on any book with a cover larger than 2 feet tall or wide, or any book with a cover smaller than 6 inches tall or wide. The book cover does not protect from water or acid. This item was created by wizards to protect tomes while traversing the Plane of Fire.

name: Weatherproof Tinderbox
description:
This small magical container holds flint, fire steel, and tinder used to kindle a fire. Any normal flint, steel and tinder that is placed in the box for over an hour will become magically enhanced, allowing anyone to use the items to start a fire in poor weather conditions, as long as oxygen is present (will not work underwater). A fire started using the tinderbox is considered magical. The fire will last for 8 hours, unless it is completely doused with water or deprived of oxygen (i.e. covered with a blanket). The fire will not grow larger than the size of a campfire (if kindling is available). It will protect anyone sitting within 15 feet of the fire from
non-magical
weather effects (they will not get wet from rain, feel the cold of snow, and be protected from harsh winds). It will
not
protect from
magical weather effects
. After 8 hours, the magical fire turns into a regular fire, and is susceptible to the elements, and can potentially spread (if kindling is available)

name: Sponge of Absorption
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a rounded rectangular, tan-yellow sponge, about 9 inches long, 6 inches wide, and 4 inches deep. Using an action, you can use the sponge to absorb water. The sponge can only absorb 119,680 ounces or 935 gallons of water a day (5-foot cube). Most of the water absorbed by this sponge is teleported to the Plane of Water. After the sponge absorbs over 2 cups of water, squeezing it will release only 2 cups of water (and no more). If the sponge is destroyed (AC 12, 12hp), it will burst, filling the area with 30x30 feet of water.

name: Pot of Cooking
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This item appears as a cylindrical, black, cast-iron, kettle pot, with two side handles, and a lid with a handle. It is 18 inches in diameter, 12 inches deep, and can hold about 8 gallons of liquid. When the lid is placed on the pot, and a command word is spoken, the pot will heat up it's contents in 5 rounds (no fire is needed). The pot only works if its lid is secured on top, and the pot contains non-living material.

name: Fork of Poultry
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is an iron fork, with a wooden handle branded with the image of a chicken. Anything eaten with this fork tastes like chicken.

name: Robe of Colors
description:
This is a soft wool robe, of a solid color (DM choice). Once per day, using a bonus action, the wearer can will the robe to change color (player choice). The robe can only change to solid colors. It does
not
change the fabric, nor add patterns.

name: Mug of Frost
description: This is a wooden mug engraved with icicles and snowflakes, with a cast-iron handle. Any liquid placed in the mug is chilled down to 40°F (4°C). If a liquid with a high cooling point solidifies in the mug (i.e., lava), it will render the mug useless.

name: Mug of Warmth
description: This is a brass mug engraved with the symbol of a teapot on a campfire. Any liquid placed in the mug is warmed to 140°F (60°C). If a liquid with a high cooling point solidifies in the mug (i.e., lava), it will render the mug useless.

name: Glow Stone
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a small, round rock, about 3 inches in diameter. The rock has a permanent light spell cast on it and always sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The color of the light is determined by the DM (or the mage that created the rock).

name: Magician's Hat
description:
This is a black top hat with a red ribbon wrapped around its base. The hat grants the following abilities:
You can use the hat as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells.
Using an action, you can place a tiny, unaggressive beast into the hat (i.e. rabbit), and it will disappear. It is held in stasis, not requiring air or food.
Using an action, you can remove a tiny, unaggressive beast from the hat (that had been placed into the hat previously)
As a bonus action, you can remove the hat and cause 5 butterflies to fly out from the interior.
As a bonus action, while wearing the hat, you can cause a brief flash of light. Any creature that can see within 5 feet must make a DC 12 Constitution Save. On a failure, the creature is distracted, allowing the hat's wearer to
Disengage
.

name: Hourglass of Eternity
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a small hourglass with a wooden frame, about 4 inches tall. The hourglass is empty until someone holding it tells it how much time to keep track of. Once the hourglass starts running, it will not stop until the sands run out or until someone gives it the command to stop (not always known, DM discretion).

name: Chair of Collapsing
description: This is a wooden chair that can be manually folded using an action. As it is being folded, it magically reduces in size -- ending up as a 1-foot square, 1-inch deep board.

name: Deck of Many Thongs
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
A deck of playing cards with images of naked women. The images are slightly animated -- subtle smile, sultry wink, tilting head.

name: Torch of Darkvision
description: This appears to be an unlit torch, with oily black cloth wrapped around its head. When held in your hand, anyone within 30 feet of the torch has darkvision. The darkvision extends 30 feet from the torch, not from the affected creatures. Example: If a human is standing 20 feet from the torch, they have darkvision for another 10 feet away from the torch.

name: Bedroll of Wetting
description: This looks like a simple bedroll. After a creature sleeps in the bedroll for 4 hours, a wet spot emerges.

name: Breadbox of Toasting
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a small, wooden box with a slidable wooden door. It is about 16 inches wide and 9 inches high and deep. When bread is placed into the box and the door is closed, the bread becomes toasted in 1 round. The box only works when bread is placed in the box. If anything else is placed in the box, it will not work.

name: Mug of Drunkenness
description: This is an iron mug engraved with images of lively dwarves drinking in a tavern. Any drink poured into the mug looks and tastes like that beverage. However, drinking a beverage from the mug has the effect of a strong dwarven ale. If any beverage is poured out of the mug (and not drunk), it reverts to its original state (i.e., non-alcoholic).

name: Boots of Mud Resistance
description: This is a pair of fine, sturdy, brown leather traveling boots with a fine shine. No matter how much mud, dirt and filth you walk through, these boots always stay clean. Note: The magic only protects the boots.

name: Ring of Perfect Manicure
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
Anyone wearing this ring wakes up with perfectly manicured nails. Once per day, you can use an action to magically paint your nails any color.

name: Immovable Hat
description: This is a fine hat (DM choice on the hat's style). The hat can only be taken off by the wearer. A DC 18 Strength check is required for someone else to remove it.

name: Harness of Pleasant Odor
description: This is a leather horse's harness etched with floral imagery. While harnessed on a horse, the horse has a pleasant odor.

name: Bedpan of Teleportation
description: A small metal bowl with a seated tip. When someone is seated on the bowl, any refuse (only creature excrement) is magically teleported away. Other items remain in the bowl.

name: Boots of Graceful Dancing
description: These are blue suede boots with a leather cuff at the top. When wearing these boots, you get a +5 performance when dancing. This bonus does not apply to any acrobatics you wish to perform during your routine.

name: Shoes of Perpetual Shininess
description: These are a pair of fine leather shoes, which gleam as if recently shined. They retain this shine and never need to be polished. While the shoes can get covered in dirt and mud, that can easily be brushed off, revealing the shiny shoes underneath.

name: Skipping Stone of Returning
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a flat stone about 2 inches wide. When you throw the rock across a wet surface, the rock will skip 1d6+2 times before making a u-turn and skipping back. When it returns, you must make a DC 10 Dexterity check. On a success, you catch the rock. On a failure, the rock hits you, and you take 1 damage.

name: Lute of Tuning
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a finely made lute. As long as the strings are attached (and not cut or removed), the lute will be in tune and never need to be adjusted.

name: Quill of Grammar
description: This is a magical feather quill. When writing with this quill, the pen causes you to write with perfect grammar (no save required). If you wish to write using incorrect grammar on purpose, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom save. On a failure, you are forced to write in perfect grammar.

name: Quill of Ink
description: This is a finely made, white feather quill with a black stem. When writing with this quill, the tip is constantly wet with ink. You never need to apply ink to write.

name: Cards of Shuffling
description: This is a simple deck of cards. While holding the cards, you can will the cards to shuffle on their own. Alternatively, if the cards are within 30 feet, you can speak a command word, and the cards will shuffle on their own.

name: Stamp of Unending Ink
description: This is an iron seal of a symbol (DM's choice), with a wooden handle. It also has a wooden cap that covers the iron seal. When the seal is placed near a surface, the seal magically becomes covered with ink, allowing it to be used as a stamp.

name: Cork of Freshness
description: This is a standard cylindrical cork. When placed on a bottle, the contents of the bottle won't spoil. If the contents are already spoiled, the cork will NOT unspoil the contents — the contents just won't spoil any further. This item is prized by vintners and wineries.

name: Invisible Ring
description: When this silver band is placed on your finger, the ring becomes invisible. This ring does not turn the wearer invisible, only the ring. The ring becomes visible when it is removed from your finger, or the wearer is dead.

name: Amulet of Anti-Hangovers
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a round copper amulet on a thin chain. When worn, it will stave off the
aftereffects
of alcohol. It will not prevent inebriation, nor will it prevent any other illnesses.

name: Candle of Clean Air
description:
When lit, this is a smokeless candle that emits a 10-foot-radius aura of clean air. The aura wards off any
natural
impurities in the air (i.e., smoke, odors) but does not protect from any magical effects (i.e.,
stinking cloud
). The candle lasts for 1 hour and sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

name: Everburning Candle
description: This is a candle that will last until it is extinguished. It can be extinguished by speaking a command word or by depriving the flame of oxygen (covering the flame, dousing it in water). While lit, the candle sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

name: Gold Coin of Returning
description: This appears to be a standard gold coin. When attuned to this coin, the coin becomes "linked" to you. After an hour of losing possession of the coin (i.e., given to someone else, stolen), it will magically disappear the moment the coin is not being watched by anyone. The coin will magically appear on the attuned owner's person (i.e., in a pouch, pocket, or backpack). The coin stops returning to its owner when they are no longer attuned (or dead).

name: Handkerchief of Silent Nose Blowing
description: This is a white, square, linen handkerchief. When used to blow your nose, it is completely silent. It does not silence any other actions.

name: Mug of Unspilled Liquid
description: This appears to be a standard wooden mug. Unless this mug is being drunk by a creature, any liquid inside the mug will not pass the rim. If the mug is tipped over, the liquid will press against the top of the mug, as if there was a pane of glass blocking the liquid from spilling.

name: Teddy Bear of Quiet Slumber
description: When you are within 5 feet of this teddy bear, you are able to fall asleep in less than 1 minute. Anyone sleeping within 5 feet of the bear has their passive perception drop by 5. They can not be woken by loud noises but will wake up if they take damage or if someone uses an action to shake them awake (a quick kick or shove won't do it). Anyone who sleeps for 6 straight hours within 5 feet of the teddy bear will be well-rested and refreshed, with 1d6+2 temporary hit points.

name: Loofah of Perpetual Soapiness
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a round, tan sponge, about 8 inches in diameter. When water is applied, and it is squeezed or pressed against a surface, it emits a frothy, soapy lather.

name: Soles of Comfort
description: These are leather insoles that can be slipped inside a pair of shoes or boots. When wearing these, your feet feel extremely comfortable, even after a long day of travel on the road. Other than comfort, they provide no other benefits.

name: Everburning Weapon
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
Upon speaking a command word, this weapon (DM choice) becomes sheathed in a harmless flame. The flame is an illusion and causes no damage.

name: Quill of Dictation
description: This is a light blue feathered quill with a dark blue stem etched with magical runes. If you are the last person to touch the quill and are within 30 feet of it, speaking a command word will animate the quill, causing it to fly up and seek a piece of paper/parchment. As long as there is paper/parchment nearby, it will write whatever you dictate. If the quill runs out of room on a piece of paper/parchment, it will call forth another blank sheet from the area (if available). If no paper/parchment is available, it will stop writing. Speaking the command word again will cause it to deactivate, often falling to the ground.

name: Everlasting Waterskin
description: This is a leather waterskin that always appears to be full of water. When someone drinks directly from it, it never empties. However, if water leaves the waterskin in any other fashion (i.e., the water is poured out), the waterskin is emptied after 4 pints are gone. When the waterskin is emptied in this fashion, it will refill itself after 24 hours have passed.

name: Ring of Infertility
description: While wearing this ring, you are incapable of conceiving children.

name: Onyx Cricket Figurine
description: This is a small, carved onyx figurine of a cricket. When within 60 feet of the cricket, it can be activated by speaking (or whispering) a command word. Once activated, the cricket will chirp continuously. The figurine can be deactivated by speaking the command word within 60 feet.

name: Coin of Eternal Tails (or Heads)
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This appears to be a common gold coin. One side has "heads," and the other has "tails." However, every time the coin is tossed, it will always land on "tails."

name: Incense of Perpetual Smelliness
description: This appears to be a bag of common incense that has a pleasant smell. However, once it is lit, it will give off a foul aroma to anyone standing within 30 feet. Anyone in that area will also give off a foul aroma for the next 24 hours. This foul odor can not be cleaned or scrubbed off. Only a lesser restoration spell (or greater) can remove the effect.

name: Spoon of Tastiness
description: This appears to be a simple iron spoon, with the symbol of a chef's hat engraved on the handle. Any food eaten with this spoon will be extremely tasty. Inedible, non-food items will not be affected by this spoon (i.e., dirt, lava, paper). Note: while the spoon will not change the taste of foreign substances (i.e., poison), it will not necessarily alert you of its presence either (DM discretion).

name: Eternal Chalk
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This is a cylindrical piece of chalk, about 3 inches tall and 1 inch thick (typically white, but other colors are available; DM choice). The chalk never wears out, no matter how much you write with it, and remains about 3 inches long and 1 inch thick.

name: Backpack of Concealment
description: This appears to be a leather harness with a flattened 18-inch pocket on the back. The backpack can be worn and concealed under any kind of clothing, making it unnoticeable. When the pocket is opened, the interior looks like a regular backpack (just with a smaller opening). It can hold 1 cubic foot/30 pounds of gear. Note: Sharp objects placed into the pocket have a 2d20 chance of piercing the backpack. Once the backpack is pierced, its magic is destroyed, and it can only hold 10 pounds of very flat gear.

name: Cask of Liquid Gold
description:
This is an
unofficial homebrew
magic item. It is up to the DM if they wish to allow the item to be used in a campaign.
This appears to be a small wooden barrel with a spigot. It is about 12 inches tall and 9 inches in diameter and weighs 18 pounds when full and 7 pounds when empty. The small barrel looks like it holds about 2 gallons but actually holds 10 gallons (1280 ounces or 80 pints). When the keg is emptied, it will magically refills in 7 days.

name: Bridle of Conjuration
description:
This is a leather bridle etched with images of running horses (or camels, or any other mount - DM choice). Using an action, you can hold the bridle out infront of you and speak a command word. If there's room for a large creature, an ordinary riding horse will manifest in the bridle wearing a riding saddle (you choose the horse's color - but it must be a standard color for a horse). The horse manifests fully fed and rested, but will need food, water and rest like any other horse as time passes. Note: The horse will not manifest with any saddlebags or barding.
At any time, while holding the reigns, the horse can be summoned back "into the bridle." Any equipment that as added to the horse (barding, saddle bags), will fall to the ground when it disappears. The horse cannot be summoned again until 24 hours have passed. If the horse is killed, its body will disappear, and can not be summoned again for 7 days.

name: Everlasting Provisions
description: This is a small leather sack, containing 4 rations. If any of the sack's rations are eaten, then in 24 hours, 1 new ration will be added to the sack per day until there are 4 rations in the sack again. If a ration is removed and not eaten, the sack will not replace that ration for 1 month's time (making stockpiling difficult).

name: Cup of Purity
description: A small, ceramic drinking cup, made of unicorn ivory. It has the power to instantly neutralize any kind of poison, toxin, or venom that's poured into it, whether natural or alchemically created, and turn it into clear drinkable water. If any liquid poured into the cup contains poisonous elements, they will also be neutralized and the poured liquid will remain.

name: Feather Boat
description:
This appears to be a long swan feather, with a stem engraved with tiny arcane symbols. When the feather is placed in a body of water larger than 15-feet square, the feather transforms into a white rowboat engraved with feather textures and design. The boat is 12-foot long, 8 feet wide, and can hold up to 5 Medium or Small creatures (or 2 Large creatures). After 12 hours, or when its user wills it (as an action), the boat returns to its feather form.
The boat can be rowed to move 1.5 miles per hour. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour). This vehicle can't be rowed against any significant current, but can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. When in boat form, it weighs 80 pounds, in case adventurers carry it over land.

name: Slippers of Warmth
description: These are soft furry slippers, with soft leather soles. While wearing the slippers, your feet feel warm and comfortable, no matter what the ambient temperature. The slippers only protect your feet and have no effect on the rest of your body.

name: Endless Quiver
description: This is a fine leather quiver that appears to be large enough to hold 12 arrows, but can actually hold an infinite number of arrows. No matter how many arrows are placed in the quiver, it will always weigh only 2 pounds. When found, the quiver will have 2d20 arrows.
